report_name: Vercel Hit by Supply Chain Attack; ShinyHunters Claims Responsibility, Demands $2M

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: Claimed responsibility for the supply chain attack against Vercel, offering stolen data including source code and access keys for $2 million on a hacking forum.
threat_actors.goals: Financial gain through the sale of stolen data.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
attack_patterns.description: The initial entry point of the attack was the compromise of the third-party AI tool, Context.ai, which was integrated into Vercel's environment.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used a stolen OAuth token from the compromised Context.ai tool to gain legitimate, authenticated access to a Vercel employee's Google Workspace account.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Service Dashboard
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely used the compromised Google Workspace account to explore accessible services and pivot into Vercel's internal cloud infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Unsecured Credentials
attack_patterns.description: Attackers accessed non-sensitive environment variables within Vercel's infrastructure that contained credentials, which were then used for privilege escalation and lateral movement.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors exfiltrated data, including source code and database information, from Vercel's cloud environment.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Stolen data, including source code and employee records, was exfiltrated to be sold on a hacking forum.

identities.name: Vercel
identities.description: A cloud deployment platform that was the primary target of a supply chain attack. The breach resulted in the theft of source code, database information, and credentials.

identities.name: Context.ai
identities.description: A third-party enterprise AI platform that was compromised by attackers. A stolen OAuth token from this service, linked to a Vercel employee, served as the initial vector for the attack on Vercel.

identities.name: Google Workspace
identities.description: The cloud productivity platform whose account belonging to a Vercel employee was hijacked by attackers using a stolen OAuth token.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A post on the hacking forum BreachForums was used by ShinyHunters to offer the stolen Vercel data for sale.

indicators.name: Suspicious Google Drive API Usage
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Suspicious Google Drive API Usage
id: 9c5b1a3d-8f9e-4b1a-8c7d-6e5f4d3c2b1a
status: experimental
description: Detects network requests to a Google API endpoint related to broad Google Drive read access. This scope was abused in the Vercel supply chain attack.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/vercel-confirms-breach-as-hackers-claim-to-be-selling-stolen-data/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-18
tags:
  - attack.t1078
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|contains: 'googleapis.com'
    c-uri|contains: '/auth/drive.readonly'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic related to the high-risk 'drive.readonly' Google OAuth scope, which was exploited in the Vercel supply chain attack.

indicators.name: Suspicious Environment Variable Enumeration
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Environment Variable Enumeration
id: e4d3c2b1-a0f9-4e8d-9c7b-6a5f4d3c2b1a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'env' or 'printenv' commands, which can be used by attackers to discover credentials stored in environment variables, as seen in the Vercel breach.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/vercel-confirms-breach-as-hackers-claim-to-be-selling-stolen-data/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-18
tags:
  - attack.t1552
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/env'
      - '/printenv'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of 'env' or 'printenv' commands, which attackers can use to find credentials stored in environment variables.

relationships.target_name: Compromise Software Dependencies and Development Tools
